message:
feat: live resource telemetry collection and ops ingestion pipeline

Replace stub zero-valued ResourceCollectorSample with actual /proc-based
CPU jiffies, RSS, and I/O byte collection in TwoTierSearcher. Add
CpuJiffiesSnapshot state tracking with Mutex for delta-based CPU%
estimation across resource emission intervals.

Add ResourceSampleRecord::from_resource_envelope() in frankensearch-ops
storage layer to ingest canonical telemetry envelopes into the ops
database with full schema-version and event-type validation.

Update telemetry_adapter_common.sh helper with extended field assertions
for the richer resource sample payloads.

Sync beads metadata for new issue records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2w7x","title":"Ship frankensearch-fsfs as a batteries-included standalone search tool","description":"Ship frankensearch as a usable standalone search tool.

## Current State (2026-02-14 assessment)

The core search pipeline is implemented and well-tested:
- 12 workspace crates, ~2,100 passing tests, 0 failures (excluding frankensearch-fsfs)
- 2-tier hybrid search works: potion-128M fast tier (~0.57ms, 256d) + MiniLM-L6-v2 quality tier (~128ms, 384d)
- Lexical search via Tantivy BM25, fused with semantic via RRF (K=60)
- Progressive iterator: fast results immediately, refined results if quality tier completes in time
- Storage layer (FrankenSQLite-backed document store, job queue, content-hash dedup)
- Durability layer (RaptorQ FEC sidecars for corruption detection/repair)
- Ops TUI (ratatui-based fleet monitoring, lifecycle tracking, telemetry dashboards)
- FSVI binary vector index format with f16 quantization and HNSW
- Extensive test coverage across core, fusion, index, embed, lexical, rerank, storage, durability, ops, tui

## Gap Analysis

Despite strong internals, frankensearch cannot be used as a standalone tool today:

1. **frankensearch-fsfs doesn't compile** — serde lifetime error in lifecycle.rs blocks the CLI
2. **No model auto-download** — embedder traits expect ONNX models already on disk; no download mechanism
3. **No packaged binary** — no `cargo install` support, no default config for cold-start
4. **CLI not validated end-to-end** — individual modules tested but no proof the full index→search→display pipeline works
5. **No user documentation** — extensive internal docs/beads but no README quick-start or getting-started guide

## Goal

Close the gap so that a developer can: `cargo install frankensearch && frankensearch index ./my-project && frankensearch search "how does auth work"` — and get useful results within 60 seconds of first contact.

## Tiers

- **T0: Fix what's broken** — compile errors, workspace hygiene
- **T1: Model management** — auto-download, caching, integrity
- **T2: End-to-end CLI** — index, search, explain, watch, defaults
- **T3: Packaging** — cargo install, error UX, platform support
- **T4: Documentation** — README, architecture, examples
- **T5: Integration testing** — e2e validation, performance baselines, CI

## Cross-Epic Dependencies

- Depends on bd-3un (CLOSED): core search pipeline
- Depends on bd-3w1: FrankenSQLite storage integration (partially complete)
- Relates to bd-2hz: existing FSFS workstream (this epic subsumes the "ship it" subset)
- Relates to bd-2yu: ops TUI workstream (parallel effort, not blocking)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:37.300706430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:44:30.511136328Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:44:30.511106351Z","close_reason":"All child ship beads closed; fsfs check/clippy/tests passing via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","milestone","ship"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:18:43.641468457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1346,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Philosophy

This epic follows DCG (Destructive Command Guard) as the gold standard for developer
tool UX. DCG's install.sh is 2117 lines of beautifully crafted bash that handles:
platform detection, agent auto-detection (7 AI agents), SHA-256 verification, gum-based
beautiful output with ANSI fallback, shell completion auto-installation, auto-update
with rollback, and XDG-compliant configuration. We replicate every one of these patterns.

## The 60-Second Rule

A developer should go from zero to useful search results in under 60 seconds:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://frankensearch.dev/install.sh | bash    # 10s (binary download)
fsfs index ./my-project                                    # 30s (auto-downloads models, indexes)
fsfs search "how does auth work"                           # 1s (search with colored results)
```

## Tier Execution Strategy

- **T0 (Fix)**: Do first. Nothing else can proceed until fsfs compiles.
- **T1 (Models)**: Do second. Auto-download is the key enabler for zero-config.
- **T2 (CLI)**: Do third. Wire the actual index/search commands.
- **T3 (Installer)**: Do fourth. The curl-bash experience.
- **T4 (Update)**: Can be deferred. Nice-to-have for v1.
- **T5 (Packaging)**: Partially parallel with T2-T3. cargo install + cross-platform.
- **T6 (Docs)**: Can start anytime. README should be written early for clarity.
- **T7 (Testing)**: Continuous. E2E test is the ship gate.

## Critical Path

bd-2w7x.1 (compile fix) -> bd-2w7x.2 (workspace check) -> bd-2w7x.4 (model cache)
-> bd-2w7x.5 (manifest) -> bd-2w7x.6 (auto-download) -> bd-2w7x.9 (index command)
-> bd-2w7x.10 (search command) -> bd-2w7x.37 (e2e test)

This is the minimum viable path to a working product. Everything else is polish.

## Cross-Epic Dependencies

- **bd-3un (CLOSED)**: Core search pipeline. This epic builds the CLI on top of it.
- **bd-3w1 (partial)**: FrankenSQLite storage. The storage layer is needed for index
  persistence but can use in-memory fallback if not complete.
- **bd-2hz (in progress)**: FSFS feature workstream. This epic subsumes the "ship it"
  portion. bd-2hz continues for advanced features (TUI, performance, testing).
- **bd-2yu (in progress)**: Ops TUI. Parallel effort, not blocking shipping.

## asupersync Constraint (PROJECT-WIDE)

ALL async code must use asupersync. Tokio, reqwest, and hyper are FORBIDDEN.
This affects model download (bd-2w7x.6), update check (bd-2w7x.25), and any
other network operation. See MEMORY.md "Asupersync Integration" section.

## What "Done" Looks Like

1. `cargo install frankensearch-fsfs` works
2. `curl ... | bash` installs the binary
3. `fsfs index . && fsfs search "foo"` returns relevant results
4. Models are auto-downloaded on first use
5. README exists with quick-start instructions
6. CI pipeline passes all tests
7. E2E test demonstrates recall quality

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.1","title":"Fix FSFS lifecycle.rs serde lifetime compile error","description":"The frankensearch-fsfs crate fails to compile due to a serde Deserialize lifetime error in lifecycle.rs around the DiskBudgetSnapshot field. The error is: 'lifetime may not live long enough — requires that de must outlive static'. This is likely a missing #[serde(borrow)] or a struct containing owned types that need DeserializeOwned instead of Deserialize<'de>. This is the single blocking issue preventing the entire CLI from compiling. Another concurrent agent introduced this regression. The fix is probably one line — either change the derive to use DeserializeOwned, add a lifetime bound, or restructure the DiskBudgetSnapshot type. Must compile with cargo check --workspace --all-targets before closing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"QuietAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:51.535009972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:19.231500412Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:41.939842960Z","close_reason":"Already fixed upstream; fsfs crate compiles successfully on verification run","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["blocking","bug","fsfs","t0-fix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:51.535009972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1305,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Root Cause Analysis

The compile error is in `crates/frankensearch-fsfs/src/lifecycle.rs:179`. The serde
Deserialize derive on DiskBudgetSnapshot has a lifetime conflict where `'de` must
outlive `'static`. This is a known serde pattern issue — happens when:

1. A struct derives `Deserialize<'de>` but contains a field whose type only implements
   `DeserializeOwned` (i.e., `Deserialize<'static>`)
2. A borrowed field (like `&'de str`) appears alongside an owned field that has a
   `'static` bound somewhere in its type hierarchy

## Fix Strategy (pick one)

- **Option A**: Change the derived trait from `Deserialize<'de>` to `DeserializeOwned`
  by adding `#[serde(bound(deserialize = ""))]` on the offending field
- **Option B**: Ensure all inner types use owned strings (`String`) rather than
  borrowed (`&str`) — check DiskBudgetSnapshot and its transitive field types
- **Option C**: Add `#[serde(borrow)]` if the type genuinely borrows from input

## Validation

After fixing, run: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` — must pass clean.
Then `cargo test -p frankensearch-fsfs` to verify tests still compile and pass.

## Context

This regression was introduced by a concurrent agent writing lifecycle.rs. The entire
frankensearch-fsfs binary crate is blocked on this single issue. All other 11 workspace
crates compile and pass 2,100+ tests. This is the #1 priority blocker for the ship epic.

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.10","title":"fsfs search <query> returns results end-to-end","description":"Validate and fix the complete search pipeline: fsfs search 'how does auth work' should (1) parse the query (ParsedQuery with classification), (2) embed the query using potion-128M, (3) search the FSVI vector index for semantic candidates, (4) search the Tantivy index for lexical candidates, (5) fuse results via RRF, (6) optionally refine with MiniLM-L6-v2 quality embedder, (7) optionally rerank with FlashRank cross-encoder, (8) display results in a human-readable format with file paths, relevance scores, and text snippets. The default output should be pretty-printed and colored (not JSON — that's the --format json mode). Must handle: empty results (helpful message), no index (tell user to run 'fsfs index' first), single-word queries, multi-word natural language queries, identifier queries (camelCase, snake_case). Exit code 0 on success, even if zero results.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"EmeraldFrog","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:57.300069090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:40:58.027124711Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:40:58.027091679Z","close_reason":"Completed runtime search wiring and validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","critical","e2e","feature","t2-cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:57.300069090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:29:09.328863142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:55.077613551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1315,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: `fsfs search <query>` Command

The core search experience. Must return useful results instantly:

```bash
$ fsfs search "how does authentication work"
  1. src/auth/middleware.rs:45       0.87  JWT validation middleware
  2. src/auth/login.rs:12           0.82  Login handler with password hashing
  3. src/config/security.rs:89      0.74  Security configuration and token settings
  4. docs/auth.md:1                 0.71  Authentication architecture overview
```

## Search Pipeline (existing, just needs wiring)

1. Parse query → `ParsedQuery` (frankensearch-core/parsed_query.rs)
2. Classify query → `QueryClass` (core/query_class.rs) for adaptive budgets
3. Fast-tier semantic search (potion-128M) + BM25 lexical search
4. RRF fusion (K=60) of semantic + lexical results
5. Optional quality-tier refinement (MiniLM-L6-v2) with time budget
6. Display results with snippet highlighting

## Output Format

Default (TTY): colored output with rank, file:line, score, snippet
Pipe mode (non-TTY): tab-separated path:line\tscore\tsnippet (machine-parseable)
JSON mode (--json): full structured output with all metadata

## CLI Interface

```
fsfs search <QUERY> [OPTIONS]
  --index-dir <PATH>     Search index location (default: auto-detect from cwd)
  --limit <N>            Max results (default: 10)
  --format <FMT>         Output format: auto|text|json|toon (default: auto)
  --no-refine            Skip quality tier refinement
  --explain              Include score breakdown (see bd-2w7x.12)
```

## Index Auto-Detection

Search should auto-detect the index by walking up from cwd looking for `.frankensearch/`
directory, similar to how git finds `.git/`. If no index found, suggest running
`fsfs index` first.

## Existing Code

- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/query_execution.rs` — has `QueryExecutionOrchestrator`
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/query_planning.rs` — has `QueryPlanner` with intent classification
- `frankensearch-fusion/src/searcher.rs` — has `TwoTierSearcher::search()`
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/output_schema.rs` — has `OutputEnvelope` for structured output
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/adapters/format_emitter.rs` — has format emission functions
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:22:26Z"},{"id":1374,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Revision: Entry Point Dependency Added

bd-2w7x.10 now depends on bd-2w7x.28 (binary entry point). The search command needs
the clap dispatch to exist before it can be wired in.

## Revision: Tracing Integration

Search operations must emit structured tracing for every phase:
- `query_parse` span: raw query, parsed query, query class
- `fast_search` span: semantic results count, lexical results count, latency
- `fusion` span: RRF K, blend factor, fused results count
- `quality_refine` span (optional): quality embeddings, latency, timeout status
- `rerank` span (optional): reranker model, score distribution
- `display` span: format, results rendered, total latency

## Revision: Status Command Integration

The new bd-2w7x.48 (fsfs status) complements this bead. Where search USES the index,
status REPORTS on the index. Both need the entry point scaffold and config defaults.

## Delivered

### SDK interfaces
- Added `HostAdapter` trait with stable interfaces for:

1. TUI Framework:
   Use ratatui + crossterm (consistent with FrankenTUI heritage).
   ratatui for layout/rendering, crossterm for terminal I/O.
   Both are in the existing dependency ecosystem.

2. Core Types:
   pub trait Screen: Send {
       fn id(&self) -> &'static str;
       fn title(&self) -> &str;
       fn category(&self) -> ScreenCategory;
       fn render(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, state: &AppState);
       fn handle_event(&mut self, event: &Event, state: &mut AppState) -> EventResult;
       fn on_focus(&mut self, state: &AppState);
       fn on_blur(&mut self);
   }

   pub struct ScreenRegistry {
       screens: IndexMap<&'static str, Box<dyn Screen>>,
       active: &'static str,
       history: Vec<&'static str>, // for back-navigation
   }

   pub enum ScreenCategory { Fleet, Search, Index, Resource, Analytics, Settings }

   pub struct AppShell {
       registry: ScreenRegistry,
       status_bar: StatusBar,
       command_palette: Option<CommandPalette>,
       data_source: Box<dyn DataSource>,
   }

3. DataSource Trait (Decoupling from bd-2yu.4.3):
   pub trait DataSource: Send {
       fn fleet_snapshot(&self) -> FleetSnapshot;
       fn search_stream(&self) -> Box<dyn Iterator<Item = SearchEvent>>;
       fn query_metrics(&self, window: TimeWindow) -> MetricsResult;
   }

   This trait allows the shell to be developed and tested with a
   MockDataSource before the real bd-2yu.4.3 query API exists.
   The concrete FrankenSQLiteDataSource implements DataSource
   and is wired in when feature = "storage" is enabled.

4. Status Bar Content:
   Left: active screen title + category icon
   Center: connection status (N instances discovered, M healthy)
   Right: current time + resource summary (CPU% / RAM MB)
   Update: refresh on each frame tick (default 4 fps)

5. Navigation:
   Tab key: cycle through categories
   Number keys (1-9): jump to screen by position in current category
   Ctrl+P: open command palette
   ?: open help overlay
   Escape: close overlay or go back
   q: quit (with confirmation if unsaved state)
","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:09:19Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2yu.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DEPENDENCY NOTE: Removed bd-2yu.6.1 -> bd-2yu.4.3

Rationale: The app shell should be buildable and testable with a MockDataSource
before the full FrankenSQLite query API (bd-2yu.4.3) exists. The DataSource trait

The concrete dependency on bd-2yu.4.3 is moved to the individual dashboard
screen beads (bd-2yu.7.1 through bd-2yu.7.4) which actually need real query data.

This shortens the serial chain from 7-deep to 5-deep, allowing shell and
dashboard UI development to proceed in parallel with the storage pipeline.

The bd-2yu.7.* beads already depend on bd-2yu.4.3 through bd-2yu.7's blocked_by,
so the query API dependency is not lost — it's just correctly placed at the
screen level rather than the shell level.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:09:41Z"},{"id":254,"issue_id":"bd-2yu.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVISION (review pass 7 - async/sync bridge for TUI):

CRITICAL: The Screen trait and DataSource trait are synchronous but the TwoTierSearcher is async (takes &Cx). The project mandates asupersync exclusively for all async operations.

BRIDGE STRATEGY (required for implementation):

The ratatui render loop is synchronous (60fps tick loop). The search library is async. The bridge pattern is:

1. Background async region updates shared state:
   asupersync::scope!(cx, |scope| {
       scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
           loop {
               let snapshot = fleet_monitor.poll(cx).await?;
               app_state.write(cx).await.update(snapshot);
               cx.sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
           }
       });
   });

2. Synchronous render loop reads shared state:
   let state = app_state.read_blocking();  // Non-blocking read via try_read()
   screen.render(frame, area, &state);

3. Event handling returns async commands:
   pub enum EventResult {
       Consumed,
       Ignored,
       AsyncCommand(Box<dyn FnOnce(&Cx) -> BoxFuture<'_, ()> + Send>),
   }

4. DataSource becomes async with &Cx:
   pub trait DataSource: Send + Sync {
       async fn fleet_snapshot(&self, cx: &Cx) -> FleetSnapshot;
       async fn search_stream(&self, cx: &Cx) -> impl Stream<Item = SearchEvent>;
       async fn query_metrics(&self, cx: &Cx, window: TimeWindow) -> MetricsResult;
   }

The AppState is shared via Arc<asupersync::sync::RwLock<AppState>>. Background tasks write, render loop reads. This is the standard pattern for async-backed TUIs.
